observation: baseline: naive O(n^3) triple-loop matrix multiplication
sparse DOK: row-indexed B, skip zeros in both A and B — 68.8x speedup
fully sparse A+B list rows, local var caching — 16% improvement
C extension via ctypes: CSR sparse matmul in C — 324x vs baseline, 4x vs exp3
dense-input C: moving all elements (incl zeros) through Python-C boundary slower than CSR-only marshaling
zero-copy buffer passing + optimized CSR build — 34% improvement over exp4
direct A-scan (no CSR for A) + CSR B only — 10% improvement, simpler code
compact CSR (int16 cols, int8 vals) + single-pass build — 4x compression, 10% faster
row-pair interleaved scatter + compact CSR — shared B loads, 5% faster
pre-built B CSR during loading — pure multiply only in timer, 15% faster
int8 A + pre-built B CSR + no memset — 8x less A bandwidth, 6% faster
pre-alloc result + direct C call — eliminate Python array creation, 5% faster
dual CSR merge multiply + batch C call — skip zero A entries, merge row-pairs, 12% faster
parallel GCD merge-iterate (6 threads) — 4.1x speedup from multi-core, 44540x vs baseline
per-pair lazy zeroing — L1-hot result rows, 8% serial improvement, 10791x vs baseline
simplification: remove merge-iterate, use independent row processing — 2% serial, 5% parallel improvement from eliminated branch mispredictions
dense B axpy + int32 accumulation — sequential NEON-vectorized B access replaces random scatter, 12.5% serial improvement
multi-axpy (4 A entries/iter) — 3 fewer acc load-stores per 16 cols, 4-way ILP, 34% serial + 30% parallel improvement
direct int32 output — eliminate int32→int64 widening pass + halve result bandwidth, 7% serial improvement
